name: Overpass API
what_it_does: Read-only HTTP API for querying OpenStreetMap data using the Overpass Query Language (OverpassQL), enabling flexible geospatial searches for points of interest, roads, boundaries, and amenities worldwide.
use_cases: Agents performing geospatial lookups: finding restaurants, hospitals, or transit stops within a radius of a given coordinate
Extracting street network data for routing or accessibility analysis in a specific city or region
Building location-aware features that need rich, globally-sourced POI data without licensing fees
Research and data extraction workflows querying OSM for specific amenity types, administrative boundaries, or infrastructure data
not_for: Real-time or write operations — Overpass API is strictly read-only and reflects OSM data with a ~1 minute delay
Turn-by-turn routing or directions — use OSRM, GraphHopper, or Valhalla for routing
Applications needing geocoding (address to coordinates) — use Nominatim or Photon for that
best_when: Best when an agent needs flexible, globally-comprehensive geospatial data queries against OpenStreetMap at no cost, and the team can invest in learning OverpassQL syntax for precise spatial filtering.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need real-time data freshness (OSM data has propagation delays), when your use case requires address geocoding or turn-by-turn routing, or when complex OverpassQL query construction is too costly for your agent's prompt budget.

auth.notes: No authentication required for public server queries. Requests are open and anonymous. Self-hosted instances may add authentication at the operator's discretion. Public servers apply rate limiting by IP to prevent abuse.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free on public servers (overpass-api.de, overpass.kumi.systems, overpass.osm.rambler.ru). Public servers throttle heavy users — very large queries or sustained high-frequency requests will be rate-limited or blocked.
pricing.paid_tiers: No official paid tier. Commercial deployments should self-host (AGPL license) or use managed Overpass providers. Some cloud providers offer managed Overpass instances.
pricing.notes: For production agent use with predictable performance, self-hosting is strongly recommended. Relying on public servers for automated/high-frequency queries violates usage policy and leads to IP blocking.

performance.rate_limits: Public servers enforce a slot-based rate limit: each query occupies a query slot for its duration. Maximum 2-3 simultaneous queries per IP. Heavy queries (large bounding boxes, complex filters) may time out after 180 seconds. Server load varies with global usage patterns.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Overpass API error responses are plain text or minimal XML/JSON with terse messages. Rate limit errors return HTTP 429 with a brief message. Query syntax errors return text describing the problem but require OverpassQL knowledge to interpret. Not agent-friendly error surfaces.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All queries are read-only GET/POST requests against a static OSM snapshot — fully idempotent. Same query always returns the same result against the same data snapshot.
known_agent_gotchas: OverpassQL is a custom domain-specific language that requires significant prompt engineering for agents to generate correctly — errors are common without few-shot examples
Public server rate limiting is aggressive for automated use — production agents must self-host or arrange dedicated server access
Large bounding box queries can time out or return enormous result sets — agents must add result size limits (e.g., [maxsize:...][timeout:...] settings in query)
OSM data completeness varies dramatically by region — a query that returns good results for London may return sparse results for rural areas
Output format must be specified (JSON, XML, CSV) — default is XML which is verbose and harder for agents to parse
OverpassQL has two distinct query styles (union and difference blocks) and node/way/relation distinctions that agents frequently confuse
The 'around' radius filter (finding things near a point) requires specific syntax that differs from bounding box queries

security_score_components.security_notes: No authentication means no credential management risk. All data is read-only public OpenStreetMap data — no sensitive information is accessible. AGPL license source code is fully auditable. Public servers are operated by community volunteers.
